data.title: Japan's Coincheck Crypto Exchange Enters Wall Street With Nasdaq Listing
data.description: Japan-based cryptocurrency exchange Coincheck started trading on the Nasdaq stock exchange Wednesday following a strategic merger with a special purpose acquisition company.

data.title: Nvidia Adds Hundreds To Its Workforce In China To Build Autonomous Driving Tech Amid Antitrust Investigation: Report
data.description: Nvidia has added hundreds of staff in China, aiming to improve research capabilities and focus on autonomous driving; China's antitrust investigation may hinder progress.

data.title: Nordson Corporation Stock Slides After Hours As Company Projects Lower FY25 Revenue; Technical Analysis Predicts Bearish Trends
data.description: Nordson recorded a net income of $122 million for the fourth quarter of the current fiscal, which was less than the net income of $128 million in the same quarter of the previous fiscal year.

data.title: DJT Stock In Focus As Trump Set To Ring NYSE Bell Today: Here's Why President-Elect Once Threatened To Shift Trump Media From Nasdaq
data.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to ring the New York Stock Exchange opening bell Thursday, just months after threatening to move his company Trump Media & Technology Group Corp. from Nasdaq to NYSE over trading halts.

data.title: Microsoft Expects $800 Million Impairment Charge Due To GM's Cruise Exit, Analyst Says It's A 'Step In The Right Direction' For Automaker
data.description: Microsoft Corp (NASDAQ: MSFT) has said that it will be recording an impairment charge of approximately $800 million in the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, according to an SEC filing. This follows General Motors Co (NYSE: GM) exit from funding Cruise‘s robotaxi development.
